text: This article explores the particular terminology or conceptual language adopted by the sheikh M.-Said M.-Ramadan al-Buti in the book “al-Salafiyyah”. It attempts, in particular, to penetrate the meanings underlying the concepts of the “Arabian scales”, of “scientific and ideological shield” and of “compass” that form, according to the Sheikh, the fundamentals of the methodological path of knowledge (manhaj) of the righteous predecessors who had attained knowledge with sincerity of confession and with a pristine Arabic language. Focusing on the information systematised by the Sheikh in more than half the chapters of his book, the research is a brief characterisation of the scholars who adhere to the scales of sanity and those that have strayed from them and of their work. It also gives an account of the sheikh Ramadan al-Buti's position towards the issue of the limits of questioning as it reveals his attitude toward the Quranic prohibition of questioning from one side and the other hand, towards the limits of the authorial judgment of religion that the representatives of the liberal trend in the religion tend towards. This article helps to characterise the sheikh's view of knowledge and to find out from his language which expressions are flexible and require a delicate choice; as the proposed methodology by the sheikh could, consequently, neutralise the extremes and provide a middle road for the Islamic methodological approach.
